LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF EDWARD POWELL, F. CROWLEY, GREG HOLLAND, SCOTT HOUGHTON, JOHN KISER, RICHARD PUTZ AND HOWARD ROFFMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. THIS CASE IS NOT PREEMPTED BECAUSE IT ARISES FROM A DISTINCT CONTRACT. CARPENTER'S HEALTH ETC. V MCCRACKEN (2000) 83 CAL. APP. 4TH 1365. THE DEFENSE OF ECONOMIC DISTRESS IS NOT VIABLE BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAS NOT ESTABLISHED A WRONGFUL ACT BY PLAINTIFF. RICH V WHILLOCK, INC. (1984) 157 CAL. APP. 3RD. 1154, 1158. DEFENDANT HAD NO OBLIGATION UNDER THE PLAN TO REIMBURSE PLAINTIFF FOR MEDICAL EXPENSES. THERE WAS NO DEBTOR-CREDITOR RELATIONSHIP WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCSF V SWEET (1995) 12 CAL. 4TH 105. PLAINTIFF DID HAVE A RIGHT OF SUBROGATION BOTH UNDER THE PLAN AND THE CONTRACT TO RECOVER MEDICAL EXPENSES CAUSED BY A THIRD PARTY. UNDER LEE V STATE FARM 57 CAL. APP. 3RD 458, THE COMMON FUND DOCTRINE CONTROLS. LEE WAS APPROVED IN CCSF V SWEET AND THE RATIONALE IS BASED ON QUINN V STATE OF CALIFORNIA 15 CAL. 3RD 162 (1975). MR. SCHOTT IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: BELDON YEE, CSR #2647
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUL-06-2007 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
NOTICE OF NON-PAYMENT OF JURY FEES

DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE PURSUANT TO SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE OF NOV-13-2007 IS OFF CALENDAR. DISMISSAL FILED. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
